content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION

December 11, 1997


John B. Pang, Fire Chief
Meeks Bay Fire Protection District
Post Office Box 189
Tahoma, California 96142

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-97-559

Dear Mr. Pang:

This letter is in response to your request for advice on behalf of the Meeks Bay Fire Protection District regarding the provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

QUESTIONS

1. May you include in a newsletter the names of the board with a telephone number on how to reach them?

2. May you include in a newsletter the names and occupations of the board with a telephone number on how to reach them?

3. May you include in a newsletter an "organizational chart" of the district with the board members' names?

4. May you reproduce a letter in a newsletter from the board on district letterhead that includes the directors' names?

5. May you print a list of the directors' biographies for those who request one with a stamped, self-addressed envelope? What if it were printed on donated paper? May it include a picture of the board?




¹ Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109 - 18995, of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 450, Sacramento, California 95814 (916) 322-5660

File No. A-97-559
Page 2

CONCLUSIONS

1. If you comply with Regulation 18901(b)(8), you may include in a newsletter the
names of the board with a telephone number on how to reach them.

2. You may not include the board members' occupations.

3. If you comply with Regulation 18901(b)(8), you may include in a newsletter an
"organizational chart" of the district with the board members' names.

4. If you comply with Regulation 18901(b)(1), you may include a reproduction of
normal letterhead in a newsletter.

5. If the requests are "unsolicited" as defined by the Act, Section 89001 and Regulation
18901 do not apply to those mailings. You have not provided sufficient facts to answer this
question.

FACTS

You are the fire chief of the Meeks Bay Fire Protection District. Six months ago you sent
out a newsletter with a questionnaire. Many of the comments you received back asked who the
board was and how the public could find out more about them. Some of the other questions
were: "Where are their names publicized?" and "What are their qualifications?" One
commentator went so far as to ask if the board was "some private club." You did not publish any
of their pictures nor their names (with the exception of a byline to an article) as per FPPC Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

You are now writing another newsletter and would like to address the public's questions.
Specifically, you would like to know how you can inform the public as to who the board is,
while staying within the spirit of the law. You have no directors up for election, and for the past
15 years, there has never been a contested seat on the board.

ANALYSIS

Section 89001 states:

"No newsletter or other mass mailing shall be sent at public
expense."

File No. A-97-559
Page 3

ANALYSIS

Section 89001 states:

"No newsletter or other mass mailing shall be sent at public
expense."

Regulation 18901 (copy enclosed) interprets Section 89001:

"(a) Except as provided in subdivision (b), a mailing is
prohibited by section 89001 if all of the following criteria are met:
(1) Any item sent is delivered, by any means, to the recipient at
his or her residence, place of employment or business, or post
office box. For purposes of this subdivision (a)(1), the item
delivered to the recipient must be a tangible item, such as a
videotape, record, or button, or a written document.
(2) The item sent either:
(A) Features an elected officer affiliated with the agency which
produces or sends the mailing, or
(B) Includes the name, office, photograph, or other reference to
an elected officer affiliated with the agency which produces or
sends the mailing, and is prepared or sent in cooperation,
consultation, coordination, or concert with the elected officer.
(3)(A) Any of the costs of distribution is paid for with public
moneys; or
(B) Costs of design, production, and printing exceeding $50.00
are paid with public moneys, and the design, production, or
printing is done with the intent of sending the item other than as
permitted by this regulation.
(4) More than two hundred substantially similar items are sent, in
a single calendar month, excluding any item sent in response to an
unsolicited request and any item described in subdivision (b).
(b) [provides exceptions to the general rule.]"

The Newsletter

A newsletter which includes the names of the board of directors would meet all of the
criteria found in Regulation 18901(a). The newsletter would be a tangible item sent to people's
residences. The item would include references to the elected officers that prepared the mailing or
cooperated, coordinated, etc., with the sending of the mailing, namely the board of directors.
The cost of the mailing would be paid for with public funds. Also, we assume that more than
200 unsolicited copies of the proposed mailing would be sent. Therefore, the proposed mailing
would result in a violation of Section 89001 unless an exception under subdivision (b) of
Regulation 18901 is applicable to your facts.

File No. A-97-559
Page 4

Subsections (b)(1) and (b)(8) of Regulation 18901 provide relevant exceptions to the
mass mailing prohibition:

"(1) Any item in which the elected officer's name appears only in
the letterhead or logotype of the stationery, forms (including "For
Your Information" or "Compliments of" cards), and envelopes of
the agency sending the mailing, or of a roster listing containing the
names of all elected officers of the agency. In any such item, the
names of all elected officers must appear in the same type size,
typeface, type color, and location. Such item may not include the
elected officer's photograph, signature, or any other reference to
the elected officer, except as specifically permitted in this
subdivision (b)(1) or elsewhere in this regulation.
***
(8) A telephone directory, organization chart, or similar listing or
roster which includes the names of elected officers as well as other
individuals in the agency sending the mailing, where the name of
each elected officer and individual listed appears in the same type
size, typeface, and type color. Such item may not include an
elected officer's photograph, name, signature, or any other
reference to an elected officer, except as specifically permitted in
this subdivision (b)(8) or elsewhere in this regulation."

According to Regulation 18901(b)(1), you may reproduce a letter from the board on
normal district letterhead and publish it in the newsletter if you otherwise comply with
Regulation 18901(b)(1), i.e., the letterhead is not signed by a board member, no photographs are
included, the same font is used, etc. (See above.)

According to Regulation 18901(b)(8), you may list the names of the board with a
telephone number on how to reach them or include an organizational chart with the same
information, if you otherwise comply with Regulation 18901(b)(8). Listing the occupations of
each board member is not allowed under Regulation 18901(b)(8). (See above.)

You may rely on the roster exception only once in each newsletter. (Duncan Advice
Letter, No. A-93-090.) For example, you could include a letter from the board on normal district
letterhead that complies with the "letterhead" exception and include an organizational chart in the
same newsletter.

Mailings Sent at the Request of Citizens

Section 89001 and Regulation 18901 do not apply to "unsolicited requests." (Regulation
18901(a)(4).) An unsolicited request means a written or oral communication which specifically
requests a response and which is not requested or induced by the recipient elected officer or by
any third party acting at the elected officer's behest. (Regulation 18901(c)(4)(A).) An

File No. A-97-559
Page 5

unsolicited oral or written communication which contains no specific request for a response is
deemed to constitute an unsolicited request for a single written response. (Regulation
18901(c)(4)(a).) However, an unsolicited request for continuing information on a subject is
considered a request for multiple responses directly related to that subject for a period of time not
to exceed 24 months. (Regulation 18901(c)(4)(B).)

Therefore, as long as the request is unsolicited as defined above, you may send a board
member's biography or even his or her picture to an individual. Whether the "questionnaire"
which was sent out constitutes an inducement or request is a question that we cannot answer
without additional facts about the questionnaire.

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-
5660.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By: Marte Castaños
Staff Counsel, Legal Division

sections.conclusion:
1. If you comply with Regulation 18901(b)(8), you may include in a newsletter the
names of the board with a telephone number on how to reach them.
2. You may not include the board members’ occupations.
3. If you comply with Regulation 18901(b)(8), you may include in a newsletter an
“organizational chart” of the district with the board members’ names.
4. If you comply with Regulation 18901(b)(l), you may include a reproduction of
normal letterhead in a newsletter.
5. If the requests are “unsolicited” as defined by the Act, Section 89001 and Regulation
18901 do not apply to those mailings. You have not provided sufficient facts to answer this
question.
